25.reason: [The height is incremented only once when attaching to parent, but it should reflect the depth from root. The initial height is 1, and each level down should increase height by 1, but this logic incorrectly increments only at insertion point without considering full path depth.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [The height is incremented only once when attaching to parent, but it should reflect the depth from root. The initial height is 1, and each level down should increase height by 1, but this logic incorrectly increments only at insertion point without considering full path depth.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [The height is incremented only once when attaching, but it should reflect the depth from root. The initial p->high=1 is wrong; height should be parent's height + 1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [The height of the new node is being incremented after assignment, but it should reflect the depth in the tree. The initial height is set to 1, and each time we go down a level, the new node's height should be parent's height + 1, not incrementing its own repeatedly during traversal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [The height is incremented only once when attaching to parent, but it should reflect the depth from root. The initial p->high=1 is incorrect for non-root nodes; height should be parent's height + 1.]

31.reason: [Incorrectly incrementing p->high while traversing; p is the new node, not the current tree node. Height should be computed based on traversal depth, not modified on the new node during descent.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Incorrectly incrementing p->high while traversing; p is the new node, not the current tree node. Height should be computed based on traversal depth, not modified on the new node during descent.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Incrementing p->high while traversing is incorrect; p is the new node and its height should be set based on the parent's height at insertion point, not during traversal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Same issue: p->high is being incremented as we traverse down, but p is the new node that hasn't been placed yet. Its height should be determined once when inserted, based on the parent's height + 1. This logic incorrectly accumulates increments during traversal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Incrementing p->high while traversing is incorrect; p is the new node and its height should be set based on the parent's height at insertion point, not during traversal.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [Same error: incorrect height calculation by incrementing after reaching insertion point. Should be q->high + 1, not p->high++.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [Same issue: height should be parent's height + 1, not incrementing from initial 1 during traversal.]

72.reason: [This loop reads until EOF, but problem states exactly n integers. If input has only n numbers, this may read beyond or behave unexpectedly depending on input buffering. Should loop exactly n times.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This loop reads until EOF, but problem states exactly n integers. If input has only n numbers, this may read beyond or behave unexpectedly depending on input buffering. Should loop exactly n times.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [This loop reads until EOF, but problem states exactly n integers; may read extra inputs or hang if input doesn't end with EOF. Should loop n times.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [This loop reads until EOF, but problem states exactly n integers. If input has only n numbers, this may work, but if extra data exists, it reads too many. More critically, if input ends after n numbers, some systems may not trigger EOF immediately, causing undefined behavior. Should loop exactly n times.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [This loop reads until EOF, but problem states exactly n integers; may read extra or cause infinite loop if input doesn't end with EOF after n numbers. Should loop n times instead.]
